flashcards.back:
R → R1, R2 is lossless if:
R1 ⋈ R2 = R (natural join recovers R)

Test: (R1 ∩ R2) → R1 OR (R1 ∩ R2) → R2

Common attributes must be superkey of one relation

Given violation X → Y:

R1 = X ∪ Y
R2 = R - (Y - X)

R1 contains the violating FD
R2 keeps all other attributes + X

3NF:
✓ Always lossless
✓ Always dependency preserving
✗ May have some redundancy

BCNF:
✓ Always lossless
✗ May not preserve all FDs
✓ Minimal redundancy

Decomposition preserves FDs if all original FDs can be verified using only the decomposed relations (without joins).

Test: Check if each FD X→Y can be enforced in some Ri
